findings.description: Chart image is truncated at the bottom.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the full chart including the x-axis ('Day of Water Year'); the HTML rendering cuts off the bottom portion of the chart, losing the x-axis entirely.
The PDF shows standard whitespace between the introductory text and the table; the HTML displays a very large, solid black rectangular block in this space.

findings.description: Loss of merged table header structure.
findings.evidence: The PDF table utilizes merged header cells (e.g., 'Year 1' spanning across 'Events' and 'Total Days Per Month'); the HTML flattens these into separate, single-column headers (e.g., 'Year 1 Events', 'Year 1 Total Days Per Month').

findings.description: Loss of merged table header structure (continued table).
findings.evidence: Similar to the previous page, the PDF uses merged headers ('Year 3', 'Year 4', etc.) which are flattened into individual column headers in the HTML.

findings.description: Flowchart diagram converted to text list.
findings.evidence: The PDF contains a visual, hierarchical flowchart with boxes and arrows; the HTML entirely drops the visual diagram and replaces it with a nested bulleted text list.
